title: Fix Bug #1: Clear streaming buffer on session ID remap in done handler
description:
In the `done` case of `createStreamEventHandler`, when the server returns a different session ID than the client-generated one (`doneData.sessionId !== sessionId`), the streaming buffer must be cleared BEFORE triggering the session ID change callback. Without this, the streaming assistant message (client-generated UUID) and the history copy (SDK-assigned UUID) both render, producing a duplicate message on every assistant response after the first.

The fix adds three lines inside the `doneData.sessionId !== sessionId` branch, before the `onSessionIdChangeRef.current?.(doneData.sessionId)` call:
- `currentPartsRef.current = []` — resets the streaming accumulator so the next stream starts fresh
- `assistantCreatedRef.current = false` — resets the assistant-row creation guard so `ensureAssistantMessage` works correctly on the next message
- `setMessages([])` — clears the React message state so history becomes the sole source of truth after the remap-triggered re-fetch

Both refs are already destructured from `deps` at lines 66–86. `setMessages` is already in `StreamEventDeps` at line 27. No interface changes required.

change.location: lines 292–311, `done` case inside the `return function handleStreamEvent` switch statement

acceptance_criteria: The `done` case with `doneData.sessionId !== sessionId` calls `setMessages([])`, resets `currentPartsRef.current` to `[]`, and resets `assistantCreatedRef.current` to `false` before calling `onSessionIdChangeRef.current?.()`
The `done` case with `doneData.sessionId === sessionId` (no remap) does NOT call `setMessages([])` and does NOT modify `currentPartsRef` or `assistantCreatedRef`
TypeScript compilation passes with no new errors
All existing tests in `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/__tests__/stream-event-handler-part-id.test.ts` continue to pass

title: Fix Bug #3: Gate streamingStatus.model behind isStreaming in use-session-status.ts
description:
In `useSessionStatus`, the model priority chain at line 69 reads:

  `const model = localModel ?? streamingStatus?.model ?? session?.model ?? DEFAULT_MODEL;`

`streamingStatus` is never cleared after streaming ends. Because `streamingStatus?.model` is checked unconditionally, it permanently shadows `session?.model` after the first stream — meaning model changes via the dropdown (which go through PATCH → query cache update of `session.model`) are never visible in the status bar until the user navigates away and back.

The fix gates `streamingStatus?.model` behind `isStreaming` using a conditional expression. When `isStreaming` is false, the chain falls through to `session?.model`, which reflects the PATCH-confirmed value. `streamingStatus` itself is not cleared — `streamingStatus.contextTokens` and `streamingStatus.costUsd` continue to supply context percentage and cost display (lines 72–78 are untouched). The comment above line 69 is also updated to explain the gate.

change.before:
// Priority: local optimistic > streaming live data > persisted session data > defaults
  const model = localModel ?? streamingStatus?.model ?? session?.model ?? DEFAULT_MODEL;

acceptance_criteria: When `isStreaming` is `false`, the computed `model` value equals `session?.model` (when `localModel` is null and `session?.model` is set), ignoring any value in `streamingStatus?.model`
When `isStreaming` is `true`, the computed `model` value equals `streamingStatus?.model` (when `localModel` is null and `streamingStatus?.model` is set)
`streamingStatus.contextTokens` and `streamingStatus.costUsd` are not affected — lines 72–78 remain unchanged
TypeScript compilation passes with no new errors
All existing tests in `apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/__tests__/use-session-status.test.tsx` continue to pass

title: Add regression tests for Bug #3 in use-session-status.test.tsx
description:
Add two test cases to the existing `describe('useSessionStatus')` block in `apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/__tests__/use-session-status.test.tsx`. These cases confirm the `isStreaming` gate introduced in the Bug #3 fix.

The file already imports `renderHook`, `waitFor`, `createMockTransport`, `useSessionStatus`, and uses a `createWrapper` helper — no new imports are required.

**Test 1** confirms that when `isStreaming` is `false`, a stale `streamingStatus.model` does NOT override `session?.model`. This is the direct regression guard for Bug #3.

**Test 2** confirms that when `isStreaming` is `true`, `streamingStatus.model` IS used (so the live streaming display of model name still works correctly after the fix).

Both tests cast the `streamingStatus` argument to `SessionStatusEvent` because the test only sets the `model` field.

change.location: Append two `it(...)` blocks after the last test case (after line 135, before the closing `});` of `describe('useSessionStatus')`) in `apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/__tests__/use-session-status.test.tsx`

acceptance_criteria: Test 'does not use streamingStatus.model when isStreaming is false' passes: `result.current.model` is `'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'`, not `'claude-sonnet-4-6'`
Test 'uses streamingStatus.model while isStreaming is true' passes: `result.current.model` is `'claude-opus-4-6'`
All three existing tests in the file continue to pass
No TypeScript errors introduced (the `as SessionStatusEvent` cast satisfies the type)

title: Add regression tests for Bug #1 in stream-event-handler.test.ts
description:
Create a new test file `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/__tests__/stream-event-handler-remap.test.ts` with two test cases covering the Bug #1 fix.

The existing file `stream-event-handler-part-id.test.ts` in the same directory already demonstrates the correct pattern: import `createStreamEventHandler` from `'../stream-event-handler'`, define a `createMinimalDeps()` helper that constructs all required `StreamEventDeps` fields as `vi.fn()` stubs and `{ current: ... }` refs, then call the returned handler function.

Follow the same `createMinimalDeps` factory pattern exactly — do not use `as StreamEventDeps` type casts or partial objects, as the interface requires all fields.

**Test 1** (remap clears state) verifies: when `done` carries `sessionId: 'sdk-uuid'` and the handler was created with `sessionId: 'client-uuid'`, then `setMessages` is called with `[]`, `currentPartsRef.current` is `[]`, `assistantCreatedRef.current` is `false`, and `onSessionIdChangeRef.current` is called with `'sdk-uuid'`. It also verifies call ORDER: `setMessages([])` must be called before `onSessionIdChangeRef.current('sdk-uuid')` using `mock.invocationCallOrder`.

**Test 2** (no-remap does not clear) verifies: when `done` carries `sessionId: 'same-uuid'` and the handler was created with `sessionId: 'same-uuid'`, then `setMessages` is NOT called with `[]` and `onSessionIdChangeRef.current` is NOT called.
acceptance_criteria: Test 'clears messages and resets refs when done event carries a new sessionId' passes
Test 'does not clear messages on done event when sessionId is unchanged' passes
`setMessages` call order precedes `onSessionIdChangeRef.current` call order in Test 1 (verified via `mock.invocationCallOrder`)
All existing tests in `stream-event-handler-part-id.test.ts` continue to pass
File follows the exact same import style and `createMinimalDeps` factory pattern as `stream-event-handler-part-id.test.ts`
